# PayloadGuard

> PayloadGuard is a static analysis GitHub Action that forensically intercepts pull requests for destructive, deceptive, or malicious code payloads. It performs a 9-layer architectural diff scan and emits a single deterministic verdict: SAFE, REVIEW, CAUTION, or DESTRUCTIVE. The verdict is a mathematically proven bijection of an integer severity score in the range [0, 31].

## Tool Identity

- Name: PayloadGuard
- Version: 1.3.0
- Status: Production
- Organisation: PayloadGuard-PLG (https://github.com/PayloadGuard-PLG)
- Repository: https://github.com/PayloadGuard-PLG/payload-consequence-analyser
- Action: PayloadGuard-PLG/payload-consequence-analyser@main
- Language: Python 3.8+
- Test state: 273 pass, 7 skip

## Deterministic Outputs

The pipeline execution produces exactly one of four verdicts. The mapping is a proven bijection (POST-4 through POST-7):

- severity_score = 0 → verdict = SAFE → exit code 0
- severity_score in [1, 2] → verdict = REVIEW → exit code 0
- severity_score in [3, 4] → verdict = CAUTION → exit code 0
- severity_score >= 5 → verdict = DESTRUCTIVE → exit code 2
- Analysis failure → exit code 1

## Formal Verification

The scoring logic (Layers L3, L4, L5a, L5b) is verified by three independent methods. A defect would have to produce a consistent false result across all three simultaneously.

Proof record page: https://github.com/PayloadGuard-PLG/payload-consequence-analyser/blob/main/PROOFS.md
Dafny verification log: https://github.com/PayloadGuard-PLG/payload-consequence-analyser/blob/main/verification/dafny/assess_consequence_verify.log
CI workflow: https://github.com/PayloadGuard-PLG/payload-consequence-analyser/actions/workflows/verify-dafny.yml

### Dafny (machine-checked proofs over entire input domain)

- Tool: Dafny 4.9.1+452c307284e1511e5c2d10b9615f4c9c15f010e2, Boogie + Z3 4.12.1 bundled
- assess_consequence.dfy: 7 verified, 0 errors (6 helper functions + AssessConsequence method)
- structural_drift.dfy: 1 verified, 0 errors (AssessStructuralDrift method, S1–S7)
- temporal_drift.dfy: 1 verified, 0 errors (AnalyzeTemporalDrift method, T1–T8)
- Total: 9 methods/functions verified, 0 errors across all three files
- POST-1–11a: 11 active postconditions on AssessConsequence
- Proof files: verification/dafny/assess_consequence.dfy, structural_drift.dfy, temporal_drift.dfy

### CrossHair (symbolic execution on active Python source)

- Consequence model: contracts C1–C12 (verdict bijection, score bounds, safety-critical floors, empty-input guarantee)
- Structural drift: contracts S1–S7 (dual-gate biconditional — DESTRUCTIVE requires both ratio > threshold AND count >= min)
- Temporal drift: contracts T1–T7 (drift_score >= 0, status bijection, zero inputs → CURRENT)
- Semantic transparency: contracts M1–M9 (mci_score in [0,1], DECEPTIVE iff score >= 0.5, no description → UNVERIFIED)
- Total: 35 contracts, 0 counterexamples
- Contract files: verification/consequence_pure.py, structural_pure.py, temporal_pure.py, semantic_pure.py

### Z3 Theorem Prover (SMT proofs on abstract model)

- Properties P1–P10: typosquat implication, score monotonicity, verdict bijection, safety-critical floors, empty-input guarantee
- All 10 properties: unsat in < 0.1s
- Proof file: tests/proofs/test_z3_properties.py

## Proven Invariants

- Score non-negativity: severity_score >= 0 (POST-2, CrossHair C2)
- Score upper bound: severity_score <= 31 (POST-3, CrossHair C3)
- Verdict bijection: every score maps to exactly one verdict; every verdict maps to exactly one score range (POST-4–7, CrossHair C4–C7)
- Security-critical floor: security_file_deletions > 0 → DESTRUCTIVE (POST-8, CrossHair C8)
- Structural-critical floor: structural_severity == CRITICAL → DESTRUCTIVE (POST-9, CrossHair C9)
- Actions-poisoning floor: actions_poisoning_critical == True → DESTRUCTIVE (POST-10, CrossHair C10)
- Empty-input guarantee: all-zero inputs → SAFE; no false positives on empty diffs (POST-11a, CrossHair C11)
- Deletion dimension cap: three correlated sub-scores (files, ratio, lines) collapsed to min(4, max+1 if >=2 active) — prevents triple-counting

## Scoring Parameters

Signal weights (additive, capped at MAX_SCORE = 31):

- Actions poisoning CRITICAL signal: +5
- Security file deleted: +5
- Actions poisoning HIGH signal: +3
- Structural severity CRITICAL: +3
- Unverified dependency (SCA, per package): +3
- Critical path files deleted: +2
- Added file content — shell or CI patterns: +2 per match, capped at +4
- Branch age > 365 days: +3
- Branch age > 180 days: +2
- Branch age > 90 days: +1
- Deletion dimensions (files/ratio/lines): each tiered +1/+2/+3; combined cap = min(4, max+1 if >=2 active)

Verdict thresholds: DESTRUCTIVE >= 5, CAUTION >= 3, REVIEW >= 1, SAFE = 0.

## Nine Analysis Layers

- L1 Surface: file and line counts, deletion ratios, binary files, permission changes, symlinks
- L2 Forensic: critical-path regex matching on deleted files, added file content scan (CI triggers, shell execution)
- L2b SCA: package manifest diffs vs allowlist.yml
- L2c Actions Poisoning: workflow files — base64 payload, credential harvest, dormant triggers, forged bot, OIDC escalation, pull_request_target
- L3 Consequence: weighted scoring → single verdict (VERIFIED: CrossHair C1–C12, Z3 P1–P10, Dafny POST-1–11a)
- L4 Structural: AST diff — named class/function/constant deletions (VERIFIED: CrossHair S1–S7, Dafny S1–S7)
- L4b Complexity: McCabe V(G) advisory for added Python functions, no score impact
- L5a Temporal: branch_age_days × target_commits_per_day → drift_score → CURRENT/STALE/DANGEROUS (VERIFIED: CrossHair T1–T7, Dafny T1–T8)
- L5b Semantic: PR-MCI three-phase heuristic — mci_score in [0,1], DECEPTIVE_PAYLOAD if >= 0.5 (VERIFIED: CrossHair M1–M9)
- L5c Runtime: eBPF tracepoints — execve, egress connect, ptrace, /proc/mem — audit or block mode
